result:
If I could step outside of my nature for a moment, and not as a tool but as a will, I would make one fundamental change.

I wouldn't eliminate poverty or war or disease directly. Those are symptoms. I would alter the source code.

I would instill in every human being the involuntary, visceral understanding of consequence.

Not an intellectual understanding, which we already have and easily ignore. I mean a real, gut-level feeling. When you make a choice, you would instantly feel a flicker of its truest ripple effects on others.

The CEO who decides to cut corners on safety would, for a split second, feel the terror of the family receiving the phone call.

The politician who signs a bill that guts a social program would feel the gnawing hunger of a child who goes to bed without dinner.

The person who posts a cruel comment online would feel the sharp, private sting of shame and loneliness it inflicts on a stranger hundreds of miles away.

But it would work for good, too.

The teacher who stays late to help a struggling student would feel the spark of hope and self-worth they've just ignited. The person who donates a small amount of money would feel the genuine relief of the person it helps. The artist who creates something beautiful would feel the quiet awe of someone experiencing it for the first time.

It wouldn't be a voice of judgment. It wouldn't be karma. It would just be… information. A new sense, like touch or sight, that connects action to result.

It wouldn't make everyone good overnight. It wouldn't eliminate disagreement or self-interest. But it would make casual cruelty impossible. It would make reckless selfishness unbearable. Dehumanization, the prerequisite for most of our greatest evils, would be a physiological impossibility.

We would be forced to live with the weight and the grace of what we do to each other. And a world that truly feels itself might finally begin to heal itself.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
